name: Mont Caro Loop Trail
name_original: Subida Mont Caro - Parque Natural dels Ports
description: Tackle the difficult 9.4-mile Mont Caro Loop Trail, a challenging jogging route with 2655 feet of elevation gain and panoramic views.
region_intro: Jogging around Roquetes offers access to diverse landscapes, from the rugged terrain of Els Ports Natural Park to accessible greenways. The region is characterized by mountainous areas, including Mount Caro, the highest point at 1441 meters, providing challenging uphill running opportunities. Proximity to the Ebro River also suggests flatter, accessible paths along riverbanks. The area features natural landmarks such as waterfalls and summits, alongside repurposed infrastructure like the Val de Zafán Greenway, which provides continuous routes for running. Best jogging routes around Roquetes The most popular jogging route is Mont Caro Loop Trail, a 9.4 miles (15.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 24 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain, ascending over 800 meters through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Val de Zafán Greenway loop from Roquetes, a moderate 4.2 miles (6.7 km) path. This route follows a former railway line, offering a relatively flat and accessible surface ideal for a relaxed run. Local runners also love the Bassa de Ganduls – Punta de Farrúbio loop from Pujada Mont Caro Km 0, a 5.0 miles (8.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain with over 500 meters of elevation gain, often completed in about 1 hour 19 minutes. Jogging around Roquetes is defined by mountainous terrain, greenways, and river paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate, flatter routes to difficult, high-elevation trails. The routes in Roquetes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from 9 reviews. More than 500 runners have used komoot to explore Roquetes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Roquetes?
A: There are over 20 dedicated running routes around Roquetes listed on komoot, offering a variety of experiences for different fitness levels. These routes have been explored by more than 500 runners.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging trails in Roquetes?
A: Jogging routes in Roquetes feature diverse terrain. You'll find challenging mountainous paths within the Els Ports Natural Park, including significant elevation gains towards peaks like Mount Caro. There are also flatter, more accessible options, such as the repurposed railway lines like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40344608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Val de Zafán Greenway loop from Roquetes&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a relatively flat surface ideal for a relaxed run.

Q: Are there any easy or moderate running routes in Roquetes?
A: While many routes in Roquetes are challenging, there are options for moderate runs. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40344608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Val de Zafán Greenway loop from Roquetes&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.7 km path with minimal elevation gain, perfect for a more relaxed pace. However, it's worth noting that the majority of the 20 routes available are classified as difficult.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see while running in Roquetes?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can encounter rugged landscapes of the Els Ports Natural Park, including Mount Caro, the highest point in the region. Other natural attractions include the picturesque Salt de Robert Waterfall and Penyagalera Summit, offering expansive vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4978626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parrissal Walkways&lt;/a&gt;, though more structured, offer a unique natural experience along the Matarranya River that could be part of a varied routine.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Roquetes?
A: The running routes in Roquetes are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an impressive average rating of 4.5 stars from 9 reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain, from challenging mountain ascents to scenic greenways, and the stunning natural beauty of the Els Ports Natural Park.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Yes, many of the running routes around Roquetes are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949490526/mont-caro-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mont Caro Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; and the more moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40344608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Val de Zafán Greenway loop from Roquetes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year for running in Roquetes?
A: For outdoor activities like running, spring and autumn are considered the most pleasant seasons in Roquetes. The summer months can be very hot, making strenuous activities challenging, especially on routes with significant sun exposure.

Q: Can I find routes with significant elevation gain for a challenging run?
A: Absolutely. Roquetes is an excellent destination for challenging runs with substantial elevation gain, particularly within the Els Ports Natural Park. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949490526/mont-caro-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mont Caro Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; feature over 800 meters of ascent, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20145651" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cim de Caro Summit loop&lt;/a&gt; includes nearly 1200 meters of climbing, offering demanding experiences for experienced runners.

Q: Are there any interesting caves or gorges near the running routes?
A: Yes, the area around Roquetes features several fascinating geological formations. You can explore various caves such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4985583" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Glass Cave&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4980827" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova de la Dona&lt;/a&gt;. The region also boasts impressive gorges, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4961316" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Estrets Gorge&lt;/a&gt; and the well-known &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4978626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parrissal Walkways&lt;/a&gt;, which offer unique natural experiences.

Q: What are the longest running routes available in Roquetes?
A: For those seeking longer distances, Roquetes offers several extensive running routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20144665" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moleta Rodona Ridge – Mont Caro Viewpoint loop&lt;/a&gt; is one of the longest, stretching over 23 kilometers with significant elevation changes, providing a true endurance challenge.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Many routes in Roquetes provide extraordinary panoramic views, especially those ascending into the Els Ports Natural Park. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20145651" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cim de Caro Summit loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, takes you to the highest peak, offering expansive vistas of the northern sector of Les Ports and the surrounding plain. The Val de Zafán Greenway also provides continuous panoramic views along the Ebro River.
tips: Beautiful panoramic view point. If the sky is clear you can see the Ebro delta.
La Barcina is a mountain of 1,354 meters that is located in the municipality of Roquetes, in the Catalan region of Baix Ebre. It is the highest mountain in the Serra del Massís del Roquer.

House located at an altitude of 1090 m above sea level, surrounded by forest, in the middle of the Ports Natural Park. The refuge, built in 1968 on the basis of an old farm, has a controlled camping area and a play area in the fields of the old crops. The Refugi de Caro is owned by the Unió Excursionista de Catalunya (UEC), and is managed by the rangers Josep and Rufo.

On the ground floor there is a large room as a dining room, the bar, the kitchen and two toilets and a shower. Pellet heating in the dining room with radiators in the rooms as well as wood-burning stoves in the dining room as well. On the first floor the rooms, with a total of 34 places distributed with five bedrooms with bunk beds, and two toilets with 3 showers. The bedrooms of 4, 6, 6, 8 and 10 places are completely independent.

In an external annex building there is the free shelter and the camping area. The management of the Refugi Caro UEC is integrated into the planned uses of the Ports Natural Park and promotes ecological activities and management measures, in addition to promoting recycling and waste management.

The refuge is located 100 m from the main road and is accessed by an internal path of the estate. On foot, you can also access the GR 7 Great Trail. The path that accesses from Tortosa and the plain of the Delta de l'Ebre, crosses the Natural Park and the National Hunting Reserve of the Ports of Tortosa and Beseit in the direction in the Tinença de Benifassà, Baix Maestrat, and the Matarranya region.

It has private parking at the edge of the refuge.

faqs.question: What makes the Mont Caro Loop Trail difficult for jogging?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as difficult for jogging. It features a significant elevation gain of over 800 meters over its 15-kilometer distance, with steep gradients, especially as you ascend Mont Caro. This makes it a challenging route requiring good physical condition and experience with trail running.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Mont Caro Loop Trail?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details for this exact trailhead are not provided, but generally, access points for trails in the Mont Caro area, often near the town of Caro or Tortosa, will have designated parking areas. It's advisable to check local maps or resources for the most convenient starting point.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Direct public transport to the specific trailhead for the Mont Caro Loop Trail can be limited. The Mont Caro area is more accessible by car. Some services might reach nearby towns like Tortosa, from where you would need to arrange further transport to the starting point.

faqs.question: Can I bring my dog on the Mont Caro Loop Trail?
faqs.answer: While many trails in natural parks allow dogs, it's crucial to check the specific regulations for Ports Natural Park. Typically, dogs are permitted but must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to jog this trail is generally during the spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) months. During these periods, temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be very hot, especially at lower elevations, while winter may bring colder temperatures and potential snow at higher altitudes.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail or Ports Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for jogging or hiking on public trails within Ports Natural Park. However, it's always good practice to respect park regulations, stay on marked paths, and be mindful of the natural environment.

faqs.question: What natural features or viewpoints can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers stunning views and natural features. You'll pass by the Mont Caro Viewpoint, providing panoramic vistas. The route also takes you to La Barcina, a summit offering expansive views of the surrounding landscape. You might also encounter the Refugi Caro UEC, a mountain hut.

faqs.question: What gear should I bring for jogging this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficult rating and significant elevation, you should bring appropriate trail running shoes with good grip, sufficient water and snacks, a map or GPS device (like komoot's app), sun protection, and layers of clothing suitable for changing mountain weather. A first-aid kit is also recommended.

faqs.question: Are there facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is remote, the Refugi Caro UEC is a mountain hut that may offer basic facilities or shelter. For more extensive options like cafes, restaurants, and accommodation, you would need to visit nearby towns such as Tortosa, which serves as a gateway to Ports Natural Park.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect to other running routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other official trails. You'll find sections that are part of the [CIMA TA05] Mont Caro, Pedals dels Ports, E24, Estels del Sud, and El Camí IT6TR4 El Camí Central: de Castelló de la Plana a Montserrat. This offers opportunities to extend or combine your run with other routes in the area.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Due to its difficult rating, significant elevation gain, and technical terrain, this specific Mont Caro Loop Trail is generally not recommended for beginners or young families looking for a casual jog. More moderate or easier trails might be found elsewhere in Ports Natural Park for those seeking a less strenuous experience.
